summary: DHH announces that Rails works well on Windows despite his personal preference for OS X and Unix systems. He credits the One-click Ruby Installer by Andy Hunt and Curt Hibbs for making installation easy. Tobias Luetke (future Shopify founder) created tutorial movies demonstrating the setup process. DHH encourages others to create similar demonstration videos through Rails Academy.
key_points: Rails was cross-platform from early on, working on Windows despite the creator's Unix preference
Community contributions like the One-click Ruby Installer lowered barriers to entry
Early Rails advocacy relied on screen recordings and tutorials to spread adoption
DHH actively encouraged community members to create educational content through Rails Academy
money_quotes: Despite my obvious OS X/*nix bias, Rails works great on Windows.
key_insight: Even in its earliest days, Rails prioritized accessibility across platforms and leaned on community-driven tutorials to drive adoption.
